primary_accounting_issue: Détermination, au regard d’IFRS 9, de la caractéristique contractuelle « solely payments of principal and interest » d’un actif financier et de son rôle dans la classification ultérieure de l’actif

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule and selected IFRS 9 because it directly governs the classification of financial assets, including the contractual cash flow test for solely payments of principal and interest; no higher-precedence overlapping governing document in the provided context competes with it on this issue.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty: the question is squarely about IFRS 9 classification and the SPPI definition, which is directly addressed in IFRS 9 paragraphs 4.1.1–4.1.4 and B4.1.7A onward.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern classification of financial assets and define principal and interest for the SPPI assessment.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify how to assess whether contractual cash flows are consistent with a basic lending arrangement and therefore meet the SPPI condition.
These paragraphs constrain the classification outcome by identifying cases where fair value through profit or loss applies despite the general classification structure.

peripheral_authority.reason: IAS 7 concerns cash equivalents and cash flow statement presentation, not the IFRS 9 SPPI classification test.
IFRS 19 in the provided extracts addresses disclosures and not the governing classification test itself.
IAS 36 addresses impairment and value in use, which is not relevant to identifying IFRS 9 classification approaches for SPPI instruments.

treatment_families.family: Financial assets measured at amortised cost when held to collect contractual cash flows and the cash flows are solely payments of principal and interest

Financial assets measured at fair value through other comprehensive income when held both to collect contractual cash flows and to sell, and the cash flows are solely payments of principal and interest

Financial assets measured at fair value through profit or loss when they do not fall into the amortised cost or fair value through other comprehensive income models, or are designated at fair value through profit or loss

approaches.label: Amortised cost
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies amortised cost as a top-level classification category for financial assets that meet both the hold-to-collect business model and SPPI conditions.

approaches.label: Fair value through other comprehensive income
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value through other comprehensive income as a separate top-level classification category for financial assets that meet the collect-and-sell business model and SPPI conditions.

approaches.label: Fair value through profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value through profit or loss as the residual top-level classification category when the amortised cost or fair value through other comprehensive income conditions are not met, or when designation is used.
